summary: Compliance firewall that gates regulated AI messages before delivery.
editor_note: Regulated AI needs a pre-send checkpoint, not another archive after the damage is done. ZeroDrift puts Anchor between agents, chat, email, docs, and marketing, with pass, rewrite, block, and escalate decisions across a public 1000 messages/day demo. Unlike monitoring tools that discover violations later, it tries to stop SEC, FINRA, HIPAA, and insurance mistakes before output leaves. The risk is whether firms trust a model-mediated gate in high-liability workflows.

product_evidence.claim: The official site describes Anchor as a compliance model that evaluates every AI-generated message against regulations, firm policies, restricted data rules, and customer controls.
ZeroDrift lists pass, rewrite, block, and escalate decisions across AI agents, chat, systems, email, docs, and marketing surfaces.

market_context.primary_user: Compliance officers, AI leaders, and platform teams in financial services, insurance, healthcare, and other regulated sectors.
market_context.differentiation: ZeroDrift sits inline before AI communication leaves the firm, instead of reviewing archives after a regulated message has already been sent.
market_context.why_now: The June 2026 seed round follows a wave of enterprise AI-agent deployment where regulated communication needs real-time enforcement.

risks.claim: The open question is whether regulated firms trust a model-mediated enforcement layer in workflows where false blocks and false passes both carry cost.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
